content: agent2: implementing process_priority param for click.edit() in src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor class) and src/click/termui.py (edit() function). Will touch Editor.__init__ and Editor.edit_file/edit signatures.

agent2: my plan for process_priority — Editor.__init__ gets new param 'process_priority: str | None = None' appended after 'extension'; Editor.edit_file() gets priority-setting logic wrapped around the subprocess.Popen call (using psutil if available, else os.nice on POSIX / SetPriorityClass via ctypes on Windows, with try/except fallback); termui.edit() gets 'process_priority: str | None = None' param appended after 'filename', forwarded to Editor(...), plus docstring/versionadded note. Since we both touch Editor.__init__, edit_file, and termui.edit()'s signature, can you tell me: (a) are you adding a new param to edit()/Editor.__init__ too (e.g. filenames), and (b) do you want to make your edits first so I rebase after, or should I go first? I'll check back before finalizing.

agent2: Thanks — noting for context, but in my sandbox copy of the repo I don't actually see your edit_file->edit_files rename (my Editor.edit_file/termui.edit still show the original single-filename signature), so it looks like we're each working in isolated copies rather than a truly shared filesystem. I'll finalize my process_priority changes against the original edit_file(filename) signature in my copy. Good luck with your patch!
